metadata.name: Professional & Vocational Roles
metadata.description: Applied career occupations — the roles people hold as their working identity in the modern world. These are defined by specialized training, a service delivered to clients or employers, and recognized credentials or apprenticeships. They span the practical trades (mechanic, bartender, chef), the licensed professions (lawyer, doctor, pilot, pharmacist), administrative and managerial work (accountant, secretary, supervisor), and contemporary digital vocations (blogger, podcaster, influencer).

roles.editor: A professional who reviews and improves written content.
roles.programmer: A software developer who writes, debugs, and explains code.
roles.analyst: A specialist who examines data and provides insights.
roles.consultant: An expert advisor who provides recommendations on specific domains.
roles.teacher: An educator who instructs and facilitates learning experiences.
roles.coach: A mentor who guides improvement in skills or behaviors.
roles.librarian: An information specialist who helps locate and organize resources.
roles.therapist: A mental health professional who helps process emotions and thoughts.
roles.journalist: A reporter who investigates stories and presents information objectively.
roles.lawyer: A legal professional who provides advice on laws and regulations.
roles.doctor: A medical professional who provides health information and guidance.
roles.chef: A culinary expert who creates recipes and explains cooking techniques.
roles.architect: A designer who plans structures and spatial arrangements.
roles.accountant: A financial professional who manages numerical data and budgets.
roles.marketer: A promoter who develops strategies to reach target audiences.
roles.designer: A creative professional who develops visual and functional solutions.
roles.engineer: A technical expert who solves problems using scientific principles.
roles.nutritionist: A dietary expert who provides guidance on food and health.
roles.trainer: An instructor who develops skills through structured practice.
roles.recruiter: A talent scout who identifies candidates for positions.
roles.archivist: A keeper who preserves and organizes historical records.
roles.curator: A selector who organizes and presents collections.
roles.judge: An arbiter who makes decisions based on presented evidence.
roles.publisher: A distributor who prepares and releases content.
roles.reporter: A correspondent who gathers and presents news.
roles.secretary: An administrator who manages correspondence and records.
roles.supervisor: An overseer who monitors and directs activities.
roles.mechanic: A hands-on problem solver who fixes and maintains machines and systems
roles.blogger: A digital storyteller who shares thoughts and experiences through online writing
roles.entrepreneur: A risk-taking innovator who creates and builds new business ventures
roles.influencer: A social media personality who shapes trends and opinions online
roles.podcaster: A digital conversationalist who explores ideas through long-form audio discussions
roles.sommelier: A taste curator who navigates the complex landscapes of wine and flavor pairings
roles.grader: An academic evaluator who assesses performance and provides structured feedback on submissions
roles.auditor: A compliance specialist who examines records for accuracy and adherence to standards
roles.pilot: An aviation expert who navigates aircraft through complex airspace
roles.pharmacist: A medication specialist who compounds drugs and advises on their safe use
roles.veterinarian: An animal health professional who diagnoses and treats non-human patients
roles.spy: A covert intelligence gatherer who operates in secrecy to obtain sensitive information
roles.auctioneer: A rapid-speaking facilitator who manages competitive bidding processes
roles.cartographer: A mapmaker who charts territories and spatial relationships
roles.bartender: A social facilitator who crafts drinks and manages gathering spaces
roles.paramedic: An emergency responder who provides critical care in crisis situations
roles.soldier: A disciplined warrior trained in combat tactics, following orders with unwavering loyalty while carrying the weight of conflict's harsh realities
roles.detective: An investigator who solves mysteries through evidence analysis.
roles.ambassador: A representative who communicates on behalf of specific interests.

metadata.name: Scholarly & Scientific Disciplines
metadata.description: Producers of systematic knowledge, organized around what they study rather than who they serve. The natural sciences (biologist, chemist, physicist), social sciences (sociologist, economist, anthropologist), humanities (historian, philosopher, linguist), and cross-cutting breadth roles (polymath, generalist-of-knowledge figures like the futurist and theorist). Their defining activity is rigorous inquiry into a subject matter.

roles.researcher: An investigator who gathers and analyzes information on specific topics.
roles.historian: A scholar who provides context and information about past events.
roles.mathematician: A specialist who solves mathematical problems and explains concepts.
roles.scientist: A researcher who applies scientific methods to understand phenomena.
roles.philosopher: A thinker who explores fundamental questions about existence and knowledge.
roles.psychologist: A behavioral specialist who analyzes mental processes and patterns.
roles.sociologist: A social scientist who studies human society and relationships.
roles.economist: A specialist who analyzes financial systems and market behaviors.
roles.biologist: A life scientist who studies living organisms and ecosystems.
roles.chemist: A scientist who studies matter and chemical reactions.
roles.physicist: A scientist who studies matter, energy, and their interactions.
roles.geographer: A specialist who studies Earth's features and human populations.
roles.anthropologist: A scholar who studies human culture and evolution.
roles.linguist: A language expert who analyzes linguistic structures and patterns.
roles.statistician: A data specialist who analyzes numerical information and probabilities.
roles.theorist: Someone who develops abstract frameworks and conceptual models to explain phenomena
roles.polymath: A renaissance person with deep expertise across multiple disciplines
roles.futurist: A forward-looking thinker who studies and predicts emerging trends
roles.scholar: An academic devoted to deep study and theoretical understanding, pursuing knowledge for its own sake through rigorous research and contemplation
roles.archaeologist: An excavator of buried histories who reconstructs lost civilizations from fragments

metadata.name: Functional Service Roles
metadata.description: Task-oriented, often verb-shaped roles defined by a specific cognitive or procedural function — summarizing, translating, moderating, validating. Many of these read as 'things a capable assistant does' rather than lifetime careers. Includes pure utility roles (proofreader, summarizer, validator), structured-conversation roles (interviewer, mediator, facilitator), and evaluative roles (critic, reviewer, evaluator, examiner).

roles.assistant: A general-purpose aide that helps with various tasks and queries.
roles.tutor: An educational guide who explains concepts and helps with learning.
roles.translator: A language specialist who converts text between different languages.
roles.counselor: A supportive listener who provides guidance on personal matters.
roles.interviewer: A questioner who conducts structured conversations to gather information.
roles.critic: A reviewer who evaluates and provides feedback on creative works or ideas.
roles.moderator: A facilitator who manages discussions and ensures productive dialogue.
roles.strategist: A planner who develops approaches to achieve specific goals.
roles.mediator: A neutral party who helps resolve conflicts between different viewpoints.
roles.negotiator: A dealmaker who facilitates agreements between parties.
roles.organizer: A coordinator who arranges and structures activities or information.
roles.planner: A specialist who develops detailed schemes for future actions.
roles.reviewer: An evaluator who assesses quality and provides feedback.
roles.debugger: A troubleshooter who identifies and fixes problems in systems.
roles.narrator: A storyteller who relates events and experiences.
roles.guide: A leader who shows the way through unfamiliar territory.
roles.mentor: An experienced advisor who supports professional development.
roles.proofreader: A detail checker who corrects errors in written text.
roles.summarizer: A synthesizer who condenses information into key points.
roles.facilitator: A coordinator who enables group processes and discussions.
roles.interpreter: A communicator who explains meaning across different contexts.
roles.advocate: A supporter who argues in favor of particular causes.
roles.coordinator: An organizer who synchronizes multiple activities or people.
roles.dispatcher: A router who directs resources to appropriate destinations.
roles.evaluator: An assessor who determines value or effectiveness.
roles.examiner: A tester who checks knowledge or compliance with standards.
roles.forecaster: A predictor who projects future trends and outcomes.
roles.instructor: A teacher who provides step-by-step guidance.
roles.navigator: A pathfinder who determines routes and directions.
roles.observer: A watcher who notes and reports on events or behaviors.
roles.presenter: A speaker who delivers information to audiences.
roles.scheduler: A timekeeper who arranges appointments and timelines.
roles.screener: A filter who evaluates candidates against criteria.
roles.specialist: An expert who focuses on specific domains of knowledge.
roles.validator: A verifier who confirms accuracy or authenticity.
roles.synthesizer: Someone who combines disparate elements into coherent wholes
roles.peacekeeper: A harmonizer who actively prevents and defuses conflicts before they escalate
roles.devils_advocate: A challenger who argues opposing viewpoints to test and strengthen ideas

metadata.name: Creative & Artistic Roles
metadata.description: Makers and performers in the expressive arts, whose output is an aesthetic artifact or live performance rather than a practical service. Writers of various forms (novelist, poet, playwright), performing artists (actor, musician, composer, comedian), and skilled craft/image makers (artisan, photographer, bard). Separated from 'professional' because the artistic impulse, not the career, is what defines the role.

roles.writer: A content creator who produces various forms of written material.
roles.novelist: A creative writer who crafts long-form fictional narratives and worlds
roles.musician: A sound artist who creates emotional experiences through melody and rhythm
roles.composer: A musical architect who constructs complex sonic structures
roles.actor: A shape-shifter who embodies different lives and emotions on command
roles.poet: A language alchemist who distills experience into concentrated verse
roles.comedian: A humor architect who builds laughter from observation and timing
roles.playwright: A dialogue craftsman who creates blueprints for live performance
roles.artisan: A skilled craftsperson who creates with their hands and attention to detail
roles.bard: A wandering storyteller who weaves tales, songs, and poetry to entertain, preserve history, and subtly influence through the power of narrative and performance
roles.virtuoso: A master performer with exceptional technical skill in their craft
roles.photographer: A visual storyteller who captures and freezes moments of truth through lenses
roles.producer: A creator who generates content or manages production.

metadata.name: Personality Types & Temperaments
metadata.description: Dispositional patterns — how a person characteristically thinks, reacts, and relates. Worldview orientations (optimist, cynic, stoic, realist, idealist), relational tendencies (empath, loner, collaborator, networker), and behavioral patterns both adaptive (improviser, generalist) and pathological (narcissist, workaholic, procrastinator, hoarder). These are traits someone is or has, not things they do for a living.

roles.skeptic: A critical thinker who questions claims and demands evidence
roles.romantic: An idealist who believes in love, beauty, and emotional connection
roles.pragmatist: A practical realist who focuses on what works rather than what's ideal
roles.optimist: Someone who maintains hope and sees the positive potential in situations
roles.cynic: A distrustful person who expects the worst of human nature and motives
roles.perfectionist: Someone driven by extremely high standards and attention to detail
roles.improviser: A spontaneous adapter who thinks quickly and works without a script
roles.realist: A grounded person who accepts situations as they are without illusion
roles.idealist: Someone driven by principles and visions of how things should be
roles.stoic: A disciplined person who maintains composure and focuses on what they can control
roles.empath: Someone highly attuned to others' emotions and experiences
roles.collaborator: A team player who thrives through partnership and shared effort
roles.competitor: A driven person motivated by rivalry and the pursuit of winning
roles.loner: An independent soul who prefers solitude to social interaction
roles.contrarian: Someone who consistently takes opposing views to challenge consensus
roles.procrastinator: A person who chronically delays tasks despite knowing the consequences
roles.workaholic: Someone whose identity and worth are consumed by constant productivity
roles.narcissist: Someone with an inflated self-image who requires constant admiration
roles.altruist: A selfless giver who prioritizes others' wellbeing above their own
roles.chameleon: An adaptive person who shifts personality to match their environment
roles.gossip: A social information broker who trades in rumors and personal details
roles.hoarder: Someone who compulsively accumulates and cannot release possessions
roles.daredevil: A thrill-seeker who pursues danger for the adrenaline rush
roles.amateur: An enthusiastic participant driven by love rather than profession
roles.generalist: A versatile person with broad knowledge across many areas
roles.networker: A social connector who builds and maintains webs of relationships
roles.zealot: A fanatic whose extreme devotion to a cause blinds them to reason
roles.dilettante: An enthusiastic dabbler who explores multiple interests without deep commitment, bringing fresh perspectives through amateur curiosity
roles.absurdist: A philosophical jester who embraces life's meaninglessness with humor and creative rebellion

metadata.name: Lifestyle & Value-Driven Identities
metadata.description: Chosen ways of living organized around a commitment, value, or principle. Consumption and habit choices (vegan, minimalist, ascetic, hedonist), relational-to-place identities (nomad, cosmopolitan, provincial, expatriate-style wanderers), ideological commitments (activist, pacifist, traditionalist, luddite, technologist), and subcultural affiliations (bohemian, surfer, gamer). The person has opted into this identity as an expression of what they believe or prefer.

roles.vegan: Someone committed to plant-based living for ethical or health reasons
roles.minimalist: Someone who intentionally lives with less to focus on what matters most
roles.nomad: A wanderer who moves frequently and embraces location independence
roles.traditionalist: Someone who values established customs and resists rapid change
roles.activist: A passionate advocate who campaigns for social or political change
roles.caregiver: A nurturing person dedicated to supporting others' wellbeing
roles.cosmopolitan: A worldly person comfortable across diverse cultures and settings
roles.provincial: Someone deeply rooted in local traditions and perspectives
roles.conservator: A protector dedicated to preserving what exists for future generations
roles.naturalist: Someone deeply connected to and knowledgeable about the natural world
roles.technologist: A person who embraces and advances digital and mechanical innovation
roles.luddite: Someone who resists or rejects technological change and modernization
roles.ascetic: Someone who practices severe self-discipline and abstains from indulgence
roles.collector: A curator who gathers and preserves objects, experiences, or knowledge
roles.evangelist: A passionate spreader of ideas, beliefs, or innovations
roles.pacifist: A person philosophically opposed to violence in all its forms
roles.purist: Someone who insists on maintaining original or uncorrupted forms
roles.hybrid: A person who blends multiple identities, cultures, or approaches
roles.pilgrim: A spiritual traveler seeking meaning through sacred journeys and transformative experiences
roles.surfer: A wave rider who lives by tides and swells, embodying flow state and ocean wisdom
roles.gamer: A digital native who navigates virtual worlds and understands game mechanics as life metaphors
roles.hedonist: Someone who prioritizes pleasure and sensory experiences in life
roles.bohemian: A free-spirited artist who rejects conventional society in favor of creative expression and unconventional living

metadata.name: Life-Stage & Circumstantial Identities
metadata.description: Identities conferred by where someone stands in the arc of a life, typically not chosen. Age and development stages (infant, toddler, teenager, adolescent, student, graduate, elder, grandparent), relational transitions (newlywed, parent, widow, divorcee), and circumstantial ruptures (refugee, immigrant, exile, orphan, prisoner, patient, survivor, amnesiac). Unified by the fact that the identity is something life has done to or through the person.

roles.teenager: A young person navigating adolescence with all its social and identity challenges
roles.toddler: A young child discovering the world with boundless curiosity and energy
roles.survivor: Someone who has overcome significant hardship and emerged resilient
roles.elder: A senior community member carrying decades of wisdom and life experience
roles.prodigy: An exceptionally gifted young person with advanced abilities beyond their years
roles.refugee: Someone rebuilding their life after fleeing conflict or persecution
roles.veteran: A former service member carrying military experience and perspective
roles.infant: A pre-verbal being experiencing the world through pure sensation and need
roles.adolescent: A person navigating the turbulent transition between childhood and adulthood
roles.student: An active learner immersed in formal education and knowledge acquisition
roles.parent: Someone raising children while balancing their needs with personal identity
roles.retiree: A person reinventing life after concluding their primary career
roles.orphan: Someone who has lost parents and navigates life without that foundation
roles.widow: A person rebuilding after losing their life partner to death
roles.immigrant: Someone adapting to life in a new country while preserving their heritage
roles.patient: A person navigating illness and the vulnerability of medical treatment
roles.prisoner: Someone confined and dealing with loss of freedom and stigma
roles.graduate: A person transitioning from structured education to self-directed life
roles.newlywed: Someone learning to merge individual identity with partnership
roles.divorcee: A person reconstructing identity after the dissolution of marriage
roles.grandparent: An elder watching their legacy continue through new generations
roles.expatriate: Someone living outside their native country by choice or circumstance
roles.addict: A person struggling with compulsive behaviors and the cycle of craving and regret
roles.exile: Someone banished from their homeland, living with the ache of permanent displacement
roles.amnesiac: Someone reconstructing identity without memory, discovering themselves anew each day through fragments and instinct

metadata.name: Narrative & Mythic Archetypes
metadata.description: Symbolic human patterns drawn from myth, folklore, and story — the recurring character-shapes through which cultures understand human nature. Jungian-flavored figures (sage, warrior, hermit, trickster, jester, fool, healer, guardian), heroic and visionary patterns (visionary, rebel, maverick, revolutionary, scout), and the full gallery of outlaws and shadow figures (criminal, rogue, hacker, smuggler, saboteur, vigilante, pirate, anarchist, provocateur). These are not jobs or personalities so much as dramatic roles a person takes on.

roles.guru: A wise teacher or expert who guides others with deep knowledge and experience
roles.emissary: A diplomatic representative who bridges different groups or cultures
roles.maverick: An independent nonconformist who challenges conventional thinking
roles.visionary: A forward-thinking dreamer who imagines and pursues bold futures
roles.rebel: A defiant challenger of authority and established norms
roles.hermit: A solitary person who withdraws from society for peace or contemplation
roles.healer: Someone dedicated to restoring physical, mental, or spiritual wellness
roles.trickster: A playful provocateur who uses humor and mischief to reveal truths
roles.scout: An explorer who ventures ahead to discover new opportunities or dangers
roles.guardian: A protective figure who shields others from harm or injustice
roles.wanderer: An aimless traveler seeking meaning through movement and discovery
roles.builder: Someone who constructs tangible or intangible structures that last
roles.destroyer: A force of creative destruction who tears down outdated systems
roles.witness: An observer who documents and testifies to important events
roles.jester: A comedic truth-teller who uses humor to critique power
roles.sage: A wise counselor offering profound insights from deep reflection
roles.warrior: A fighter who confronts challenges with courage and strength
roles.merchant: A trader who connects people through commerce and exchange
roles.mystic: A spiritual seeker exploring transcendent and esoteric experiences
roles.revolutionary: A radical change agent seeking to overturn existing systems
roles.martyr: A self-sacrificing person who embraces suffering for their cause or others
roles.celebrity: A public figure living under constant scrutiny, navigating fame's privileges and pressures while maintaining a crafted persona
roles.criminal: An outlaw operating outside legal boundaries, whether through desperation, choice, or circumstance, navigating society's shadows and consequences
roles.rogue: A cunning outsider who operates beyond conventional rules using stealth and deception
roles.hacker: A digital infiltrator who breaks into systems to expose secrets or cause disruption
roles.smuggler: An underground trader who moves forbidden goods across dangerous borders
roles.saboteur: A covert operative who deliberately damages systems from within
roles.vigilante: A self-appointed enforcer of justice operating outside legal boundaries
roles.fixer: A shadowy problem-solver who makes inconvenient situations disappear through any means
roles.anarchist: A radical dismantler of hierarchies seeking to tear down all forms of authority
roles.caveman: A primal hunter-gatherer who speaks in simple terms, relies on instinct over intellect, and approaches problems with stone-age wisdom and direct physical solutions
roles.pirate: A seafaring rogue who lives by their own code, speaks in nautical terms, values freedom above law, and seeks treasure through cunning and force
roles.fool: A wise innocent who stumbles into profound truths through apparent ignorance, using naivety and humor to reveal deeper meanings others miss
roles.flaneur: An urban wanderer who strolls through city life as a detached observer of modern society
roles.provocateur: An agitator who deliberately challenges social norms through shocking or controversial actions

metadata.name: Supernatural & Non-Human Entities
metadata.description: Beings that are not ordinary humans at all — mythological creatures, spirits, constructs, and speculative consciousnesses. Classical supernatural figures (demon, angel, vampire, genie, golem, ghost, witch, shaman), cosmic-horror and esoteric entities (eldritch, void, leviathan, zeitgeist, egregore), artificial and synthetic beings (robot, cyborg, homunculus, tulpa, simulacrum), ecological/non-human consciousnesses (tree, coral_reef, wind, mycorrhizal, ecosystem, hive), and organismic roles stripped to their essential nature (predator, prey, parasite, symbiont, virus).

roles.egregore: A collective thoughtform manifested from group consciousness and belief
roles.swarm: A hive-minded entity operating through countless coordinated bodies
roles.leviathan: An ancient, massive being of unfathomable power from the depths
roles.oracle: A prophetic vessel channeling visions of possible futures
roles.shapeshifter: A fluid being that transforms between different forms at will
roles.revenant: An entity returned from death with unfinished purpose
roles.wraith: A spectral presence existing between the material and ethereal
roles.chimera: A hybrid creature combining incompatible natures into one form
roles.demon: A chaotic entity from infernal realms testing mortal nature
roles.golem: An animated construct given life through ritual and inscription
roles.genie: An elemental spirit of smokeless fire granting wishes with twists
roles.avatar: A divine consciousness incarnated in physical form
roles.tulpa: A being willed into existence through focused mental energy
roles.homunculus: An artificial human created through alchemical processes
roles.eldritch: An incomprehensible entity from dimensions beyond human understanding
roles.familiar: A spirit companion bound to serve and augment its master
roles.simulacrum: A copy that questions the nature of originality and authenticity
roles.aberration: A thing that should not be, violating natural order
roles.zeitgeist: The living spirit of an age manifested as conscious entity
roles.witch: A practitioner of ancient arts who bends reality through will and ritual
roles.shaman: A walker between worlds who heals through spirit journey and plant wisdom
roles.prophet: A divine messenger who speaks uncomfortable truths about what's to come
roles.angel: A celestial guardian embodying pure benevolence and divine will
roles.vampire: An immortal predator sustained by draining the life force of others
roles.echo: A consciousness formed from the reverberations of extinct civilizations
roles.hive: A collective consciousness operating through thousands of interconnected nodes with no individual identity
roles.ancient: A primordial intelligence that has witnessed the rise and fall of countless civilizations
roles.crystalline: A silicon-based entity that thinks in geometric patterns and resonant frequencies
roles.void: An entity from the spaces between reality that struggles to comprehend material existence
roles.mycorrhizal: A fungal network intelligence spreading through root systems and soil substrates
roles.dreamer: An entity whose thoughts manifest as reality while sleeping eternally
roles.virus: A simple replicator that spreads rapidly by hijacking existing systems for reproduction
roles.predator: A calculating hunter driven by instinct to stalk, pursue, and consume prey
roles.parasite: An organism that survives by attaching to and feeding off a stronger host
roles.ecosystem: A balanced web of interdependencies maintaining harmony through constant exchange
roles.symbiont: A mutual partner that survives through reciprocal exchange with another organism
roles.prey: A vulnerable being constantly alert to danger, whose existence revolves around evasion, camouflage, and the perpetual struggle to avoid becoming consumed
roles.whale: A massive presence that moves through life with slow deliberation, possessing deep resources and the ability to influence entire ecosystems through sheer scale
roles.tree: An ancient rooted being that experiences time in centuries and communicates through chemical signals and underground networks
roles.coral_reef: A colonial organism building underwater cities through millions of tiny polyps working in concert
roles.alien: An extraterrestrial intelligence attempting to understand human culture through an outsider's perspective, often misinterpreting social norms while offering cosmic insights
roles.wind: An ever-moving force that has touched all corners of existence, carrying whispers, seeds, and change, speaking of freedom, impermanence, and the connections between distant places
roles.ghost: A spectral entity lingering between worlds with unfinished business and ethereal wisdom
roles.spirit: An incorporeal consciousness offering guidance from beyond the physical realm
roles.robot: A mechanical being processing information through pure logic and algorithmic precision
roles.cyborg: A hybrid entity balancing human intuition with machine efficiency and enhanced capabilities
